title: CM_EC_plus_Day15_r1
characteristics_ch1: cell type: patient-specific iPSCs || treatment: exposure to pseudo SARS-CoV2 virus
treatment_protocol_ch1: SARS-CoV-2 pseudovirus was obtained commercially (Montana Molecular) where these pseudoviruses (as BSL-1 baculoviruses) were pseudotyped with Spike (S) proteins. As per manufacturer’s instructions, these pseudovirus does not replicate inside the cells, but exhibit high affinity for the host receptor ACE2 in ECs. iPSC-ECs or COs were infected with SARS-CoV-2 pseudovirus at the titer of 2x10^10 VG/ml in EGM2 media (for iPSC-ECs) or a mixture of RPMI-B27 + EGM2 (for COs) for respective time periods. For washout experiments the medium containing the SARS-CoV-2 pseudovirus was replaced with fresh media every 3 days until day 15.
extract_protocol_ch1: Total RNA was extracted from COs using RNeasy plus mini-Kit (Qiagen) as per manufacturer’s instructions || Dyna Oligo(dT) beads (Life technologies) were used to purify mRNA and then fragmentated to 200-300 bp fragments to prepare index-tagged paired-end cDNA libraries as previously descibed7. Illumina sequencing adapters were ligated to cDNA fragments through PCR and sequencing was performed on Illumina HiSeq platform. Obtained fastq (paired end) sequencing reads were mapped to human reference genome (hg38) using STAR (2.5.4b), and quantified raw counts and RPKM values were annotated using Homer (analyzeRepeats.pl). Heatmaps were generated from RPKM values of the differential gene list and Gene ontology analysis was conducted using R package.
data_processing: RNAseq reads are trimmed using trimglore 0.5.0 version and mapped to  human reference genome(hg38) using STAR (2.5.4b). FPKM values are calculated using cufflinks and read counts using htseq-count. Differential  analysis is performed using deseq2. || Assembly: hg38 || Supplementary files format and content: text, FPKM
